Home/Blog

AI news and insights blog

Clarivex AI blog: artificial intelligence news, regulation and practical insights for business in Europe and Lithuania. Read the latest articles.

AI Politics

AI Regulation Splits Into Two Paths: What US Political Chaos and Europe’s Search for Balance Mean for Business

The latest signals from the US point to an increasingly fragmented AI policy landscape: stronger voter pressure in some areas, lobbying battles, legal disputes, and ad hoc safety requirements in others. At the same time, Europe is moving in the opposite direction—not toward less regulation, but toward making it more consistent, predictable, and strategically useful for business.

Read more
AI Politics

AI Policy Moves Into Security Mode: What the Five Eyes Warning Means for European Organizations

A rare Five Eyes warning about the near-term cyber capabilities of advanced AI models shows that AI policy is rapidly moving toward a national security logic. For organizations in Lithuania and across Europe, that means not only greater regulatory pressure, but also a need to rethink how AI tools are deployed, how access is managed, how audits are handled, and how incident readiness is built.

Read more
AI Politics

AI Policy Moves to the Infrastructure Layer: From Model Regulation to Access Control

Recent signals from the US and EU show that AI policy is focusing less on model safety or transparency alone. The battleground is shifting to the infrastructure layer—who can reach the user, under what conditions AI systems are integrated into platforms, and how governments restrict or open access to advanced models.

Read more
AI Politics

A G7 Signal to the AI Market: Technology Leaders Are Already Sitting at the Geopolitical Table

With OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google executives joining heads of state at the G7 summit, AI companies are clearly moving beyond the role of technology vendors and becoming centers of geopolitical power. For Europe, this means not only a regulatory challenge, but also a need to reassess its dependence on a small number of U.S. model providers.

Read more
AI Politics

OpenAI Under Pressure from Two Sides: Expansion, Investigations, and a New AI Policy Risk

OpenAI is simultaneously accelerating commercial expansion through its partner network, talking about massive investment, and facing a multistate U.S. investigation. This points to a new phase in AI policy: for technology companies, the biggest risks now come not only from model quality or competition, but also from regulatory speed, consumer safety, and governance.

Read more
AI Politics

In the US, AI Regulation Is Increasingly Driven Not by Technology, but by Voter Pressure

Recent signals from the United States show that the pace of AI regulation is now being shaped not only by technological progress or safety arguments, but also by clearly rising public pressure and action from state-level politicians. This matters for Europe too: in the transatlantic AI market, rules will increasingly be shaped not just by the logic of innovation, but by political responses to voter fears about jobs, disinformation, and security.

Read more
AI Politics

Washington’s Turn in AI Policy: From Free Markets to Strategic Control

Over just a few days, U.S. artificial intelligence policy has shown a clear shift: from declared deregulation toward a more active state role, stricter security requirements, and even possible government capital participation. For Europe and Lithuanian businesses, this signals that the AI market will increasingly be shaped not only by technology, but also by geopolitical decisions.

Read more
